Using all complete cases and controlling for Sex gives F(2, 116) = 10.427106, p = 6.84841e-05, eta_p2 = 0.152383. contrast_direction: Model 7 versus Model 8: intervention-related post-intervention trajectory differences beyond group mean-structure differences
extraction_note: Recomputed from the author's lavaan latent-basis models using p_tot1-p_tot5 and opt_in. reported_result: Learning condition x question type interaction: F(1, 64) = 5.18, p = .026, eta_p2 = .08
raw_variable_names: Crit_Score_Testing_old; Crit_Score_Testing_New; Crit_Score_Restudy_old; Crit_Score_Restudy_New; Lecture; questiontype_crit
model_formula: SPSS GLM Crit_Score_Testing_old Crit_Score_Testing_New Crit_Score_Restudy_old Crit_Score_Restudy_New BY Lecture questiontype_crit /WSFACTOR = Learning_condition 2 Polynomial Old_vs_New 2 Polynomial /WSDESIGN = Learning_condition Old_vs_New Learning_condition*Old_vs_New /DESIGN = Lecture questiontype_crit
contrast_direction: Testing effect for reviewed/practiced content is larger than the testing-minus-restudy difference for unreviewed lecture content.
extraction_note: Recomputed from the author's SPSS GLM target using complete cases on the four criterion-score variables. The target within-subject interaction was reconstructed as the contrast (Testing_old - Testing_New) - (Restudy_old - Restudy_New), then tested as the intercept in lm(contrast ~ Lecture + questiontype_crit) using sum contrasts, matching the SPSS repeated-measures GLM with Lecture and questiontype_crit as between-subject factors and no Lecture x questiontype_crit term. n_total = 67; Lecture counts: 1=39; 2=28; questiontype_crit counts: -1=37; 1=30. Target interaction: F(1, 64) = 5.183127, p = 0.0261602, eta_p2 = 0.074919. Follow-up reviewed/tested content contrast: F(1, 64) = 5.325433, p = 0.0242607, eta_p2 = 0.076818. Follow-up unreviewed-content contrast: F(1, 64) = 0.739777, p = 0.392943, eta_p2 = 0.011427. Question-type main effect: F(1, 64) = 19.091631, p = 4.66226e-05, eta_p2 = 0.229766. Learning-condition main effect: F(1, 64) = 0.634395, p = 0.428692, eta_p2 = 0.009815.
